demographics.occupation: employed (role affected by concentration difficulties; drives as part of daily life)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I wake up feeling like my head is in a vise, I am constantly exhausted, and my partner says I stop breathing for a minute at a time during the night.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The exhaustion has got so bad I nearly crashed the car falling asleep at the wheel, and my partner is terrified watching me stop breathing at night.

symptom_narrative.onset: gradual over about five years, getting steadily worse
symptom_narrative.duration: years of unrefreshing sleep, now severe
symptom_narrative.character: loud disruptive snoring, then I seem to stop breathing and wake gasping and choking; wake with a crushing morning headache and never feel rested
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the tiredness is debilitating — I fall asleep mid-conversation and sitting still, and I had a near-miss in the car
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the headache is right across the front of my head first thing in the morning
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worst first thing in the morning and all through the day; the snoring and breathing pauses are every night
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing really; energy drinks and coffee don't touch it
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying on my back seems to make the snoring and choking worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: terrible concentration, irritability, generalised heavy fatigue; my weight has crept up a lot over the last five years
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: it has been building for years but has never been formally looked into
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: it is affecting my job through poor concentration, and I nearly fell asleep driving

past_medical_history.how_managed: on two different blood pressure tablets but they tell me it is still not well controlled

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: weight has increased steadily over the last five years

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: two blood pressure tablets (I am not sure of the names)

social_history_volunteered.alcohol: I do have a drink most evenings
social_history_volunteered.diet_and_exercise: drinking a lot of energy drinks to try to stay awake; not very active
social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: partner is present and severely sleep-deprived and anxious from watching the night-time breathing pauses

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's own estimate, not a calibrated clinical measurement

objective_data_offered.value: runs high, around 160/100 on my home monitor even on the tablets
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported home cuff reading, uncalibrated, single readings

objective_data_offered.value: partner reports loud snoring followed by the patient completely stopping breathing for up to a minute, then a sudden gasp and choke for air, every night
patient is visibly drowsy and repeatedly dozes off during the consultation; large collar/neck and heavy build visible on camera
